excerpt: This is the nafs that spreads its wings and refuses obedience, fleeing from righteousness
source_location_hint: Discussion of the commanding soul

excerpt: Although the apparent referent is the she-camel, in truth the conscience turns toward the souls of those who perpetrated this deed
source_location_hint: Tafsir on Surat al-Shams

excerpt: The sensible soul does not depart wholly; instead sensation reverberates within the body according to the magnitude of transgression
source_location_hint: On the punishment of the grave for sinners

excerpt: Allah forgives sins committed against Himself, yet He does not forgive sins against His servants except through reconciliation of accounts
source_location_hint: Principle of divine justice regarding intercession

excerpt: Such deeds darken the essence of the soul with terrible swiftness, and retribution becomes obligatory through garments of punishment
source_location_hint: On the consequences of major transgressions

excerpt: The righteous conception ascends to the moon, then the sun; through evaporation it returns and is absorbed into pure substance, reappearing through offspring in elevated condition
source_location_hint: On the return and elevation of the righteous

excerpt: The evil imagination, created through obstinacy toward the Imams, assumes demonic form and burns the body in the grave, manifest by night-flames in cemeteries
source_location_hint: On the punishment of the grave through evil forms

excerpt: For forty days the figure circles the grave, attempting to reconnect with the body in anguished longing, striking with force that produces pain
source_location_hint: Description of grave-torment duration and sensation

excerpt: Evil forms, once separated from the body, become satans in actuality; they affect only the ignorant, and move between heaven and earth speaking both truth and falsehood
source_location_hint: On the nature and influence of separated evil forms

excerpt: Evil forms gather in the Aqṭaʿīn as curses until appearing in a single wicked person; when such a one commits atrocities, all the era suffers under his rule
source_location_hint: On the accumulation and manifestation of gathered evil forms

excerpt: The deed becomes the companion in the grave—if good, merciful; if evil, the source of torment—appearing as beasts that frighten and hurt the soul
source_location_hint: Hadith on the deed as companion in the grave

excerpt: Ḥām ibn Ḥaym, descendant of Iblis, believed in each Prophet in succession, ascending to the sun through the moon, then reappearing to believe in the next
source_location_hint: Narrative of the employed form who repented

excerpt: The heat enters through the pores of the terrestrial crust; once within, it evaporates and returns to the state it occupied in time. By analogy: a sulphorous pot with a depression at its center, containing stones, fashioned inverted like a clay vessel—its upper and lower rims sealed, its interior forming what is truly hell.
source_location_hint: Discussion of Saqqara's physical structure and thermal properties

excerpt: In this verse, the wicked are designated with the modifier 'abiding therein'; yet the righteous receive 'abiding therein eternally'—signaling a distinction between finite punishment and eternal reward.
source_location_hint: Quranic verse on the best and worst of creatures

excerpt: Within each station are seventy chambers, and within each chamber, seventy bonds. Thus one transmigrates 4,900 times within a single category before advancing. While in animal form, they are told of the crime for which they suffer; when they descend into plant and mineral realms, such knowledge is stripped away.
source_location_hint: Doctrine of transmigration and punishment in stages

excerpt: Nine planets and seven constellations orbit both earth and Saqqara. The heavens, though superficial in aspect, rotate both planarly and circularly—their motion both orbital and axial.
source_location_hint: Cosmological description of celestial bodies

excerpt: Saqqara was created before the earth; these stubborn oppressors are its components. The Messenger of Allah declared regarding Mu'awiyah as of the tyrants.
source_work: Rahat al-ʿAql or al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama
source_location_hint: On the tyrants and their place in the cosmic order

excerpt: They rise from these valleys as dust toward mountain peaks; as they reach the earth's level, they transform. The virtuous minerals transfer to plants and animals until they acquire human stature. They are invited to the truth, shown wilayat, and called to join the people of truth.
source_location_hint: On the stages of emergence and transformation from Saqqara

excerpt: Their bodies are dispatched to the Seven Realms, where they fall into successive forms, passing through the kingdoms of man, animal, plant, and mineral. They are then hurled into extremity-punishment. From there they are restored to human form for a time, whereupon the rulers calculate and slaughter them at the field of Mina.
source_work: Kitab al-ʿAlim wa-l-Ghulam or Asrar al-Nutaqaʾ
source_location_hint: On the punishment of the Imams' adversaries

excerpt: The Angels of Wrath drive them forth at the command of the Holy World. The Tenth One, the World of Nature, delegates the manager and these guardians; He arranges all things by their celestial trumpets in hierarchical rank.
source_location_hint: On the functions of angels in the punishment of the wicked

excerpt: The unembodied intellects send punishment through these devils and foul forms—piercing meteors—and when a believer or any limit-holder curses them, it affects them. The unembodied intellects broadcast their faults, curse them mercilessly, and inflict severe punishment. When cursed, they are intensified by the rays of the imprisoned sphere and by the rays of the frozen sphere.
source_location_hint: On the role of intellects and spirits in executing divine punishment
